title: Grim Dawn

notes:
GD is one of my favourite games. I wish it would work well on Steam Proton, so I can toss away Windows for good.

Like some others here on this thread, I experience blinking/strobing/flashing/flickering of certain assets (the character, NPCs, and some objects). Can't play the game longer than a few minutes because of this, therefore the borked classification.

Also the game loses a lot of FPS compared to the Windows install. Where in windows the settings are set to high in QHD resolution, now I had to turn down settings to medium and low. Also had to untick all the boxes. Even after changing these settings the game stutters and has lower FPS than in Windows.

notes: This is an updated report from one I submitted several months ago. All I did was download the game and right clicked to select 'Play Grim Dawn (x64)'. It runs perfectly with no tweaks needed. Played for about 5 hours, give or take. All settings maxed out at 1080p. Very smooth.

notes: Although this part is more of a Steam on Linux issue, I initially had a problem  with my Steam Controller not being recognized but there is an apt package, steam-devices, that allowed my controller to be seen. The first run of Grim Dawn seemed to be working perfectly w/ mouse and keyboard but functionality was low with the controller. Under the controller settings there is an option (drop-down list) that has a setting called 'forced-on' and I also forced  Proton 4.2.9 in game settings. The controller now works with the game as well as it does in Windows 10. Very good.

title: Grim Dawn

notes:
Instantly crashed or showed a black screen on launch depending on wine version and 32/64-bit architecture.

This was due to a steam overlay bug. Steam sets up a wrong order of LD_PRELOAD arguments so that the overlay isn't properly loaded. The following launch option fixed it for me: 'LD_PRELOAD="/home/username/.local/share/Steam/ubuntu12_32/gameoverlayrenderer.so:/home/username/.local/share/Steam/ubuntu12_64/gameoverlayrenderer.so:libdlfaker.so:libvglfaker.so" %command%' In addition, the game would crash every 40 minutes and would perform very badly; this was due to using Proton 4.2-9. Switching to Proton 4.11-7, it finally runs smoothly without crashes. 

Note: you need vulkan support to run this in the default dx11 mode. This means that if you use optirun, you should install the primus_vk bridge and enable it with ENABLE_PRIMUS_LAYER=1 before this will run.
title: Grim Dawn
